name: Fungal acne (Malassezia folliculitis)
dek: Malassezia folliculitis — small, monomorphic, itchy papules on the forehead, chest, and back that look like acne and respond to none of the acne treatments. Fungal, not bacterial. Different category, different protocol.
oneliner: Won't respond to BHA. Needs antifungals — diagnosis first.
bottom: The single most important step is recognising the category. Treating Malassezia with adapalene + BPO for six months is a common, expensive, and frustrating dead end.

triggers.k: Uniform 1–2mm papules, all the same size
triggers.n: The defining sign. True acne lesions vary in size; Malassezia lesions are monomorphic.
The sebaceous-rich, sweat-rich zones. Particularly common in humid climates and after gym sessions.
True acne is usually painful or sore; fungal acne itches. Patients often describe scratching in their sleep.
The diagnostic giveaway. If clindamycin makes things worse, you are not treating bacteria.

ingredients.name: Ketoconazole 2% shampoo (as wash)
ingredients.evidence: First-line. Apply to wet skin, lather, leave 5 minutes, rinse. 3× per week.

ingredients.name: Selenium sulphide 2.5% wash
ingredients.evidence: Equivalent to ketoconazole for body sites. Useful for chest and back.

ingredients.name: Zinc pyrithione 1% wash
ingredients.evidence: Gentler; daily-use alternative once cleared. Maintenance role.

ingredients.name: Itraconazole oral (clinic)
ingredients.evidence: For widespread or stubborn cases. 1-week course usually clears.

ingredients.name: Squalane / oat-based moisturisers
ingredients.evidence: Avoid esters, fatty alcohols, oleic-rich oils — Malassezia feeds on C11–C24 fatty acids.

phases.b: Ketoconazole 2% wash on affected sites 3× per week, 5-minute dwell. Switch all moisturisers to Malassezia-friendly options.
If 60–80% improved, continue another 4 weeks then transition to weekly maintenance. If unchanged, see a clinician for itraconazole.
Weekly ketoconazole wash; humidity precautions; immediate post-gym shower. Recurrence is common; expect to maintain.
